signal_title: European Commission Finds Pornographic Websites in Breach of DSA for Allowing Minor Access
risk_rationale: The regulatory signal indicates a heightened enforcement focus on protecting minors from adult content under the DSA. While TikTok's current baseline includes youth safety measures, the emphasis on preventing minor access to adult content may require additional scrutiny and potential adjustments to ensure compliance with the DSA.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: Minimum age for TikTok service is 13+ globally, with specific implementations for under-13 users in the US.
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Protection of minors from adult content under the DSA.
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The baseline does not explicitly address the prevention of exposure to adult content, which is a focus of the new regulatory requirement.

remediations.title: Enhance Content Filtering for Minor Protection
remediations.description: Implement advanced content filtering mechanisms to ensure minors are not exposed to adult content. This may include AI-driven content analysis and stricter age verification processes.
acceptance_criteria: Content filtering system effectively blocks adult content for users under 18.
Age verification process is enhanced to prevent minors from bypassing restrictions.

business_impact: Failure to comply with the DSA's focus on minor protection could result in significant fines and damage to TikTok's reputation in the EU market.
recommended_actions: Conduct a comprehensive review of current content moderation practices.
Develop and deploy enhanced age verification and content filtering technologies.
Engage with EU regulators to ensure alignment with DSA requirements.
